_comment_about_this_file: Source of truth for the scope set being submitted in App Review. Diff against future submissions to catch scope drift. The OAuth scope strings in src/services/facebookService.ts and the Facebook Login for Business Configuration must both match this list — if they drift, the OAuth dialog will fail to request a scope we're submitting or will request a scope we haven't justified, and the submission gets rejected.
submitted_at: TBD — fill in when Steve submits via developers.facebook.com

previous_submission.rejection_reason: Screencast Not Aligned with Use Case Details — previous take started after the OAuth consent dialog, so reviewers could not verify that users see and consent to the permissions before the app uses them.

scopes.name: pages_show_list
scopes.why_we_need_it: After OAuth consent, GET /me/accounts returns the user's manageable Pages. We render a picker UI so the user selects which Page to connect to SocialAI Studio. Most SMB owners admin multiple Pages.

scopes.name: pages_manage_posts
scopes.why_we_need_it: Core product value — we publish AI-generated posts (text + image) to the connected Page via POST /{page-id}/feed and POST /{page-id}/photos. Without this scope the app can generate posts but cannot publish them.

scopes.name: pages_read_engagement
scopes.why_we_need_it: Two uses: (1) the in-app Insights tab surfaces engagement back to the user, (2) the AI uses 28-day rolling engagement as a learning signal to write better posts. Calls GET /{page-id}/insights and GET /{page-id}/posts (with likes/comments/shares summaries).

scopes.name: instagram_content_publish
scopes.why_we_need_it: Most SMB users want to publish to Facebook AND Instagram in parallel from one tool. After we detect a linked instagram_business_account on the Page, we publish via POST /{ig-user-id}/media → POST /{ig-user-id}/media_publish (Content Publishing API). Publish-only, no reads of private content.

scopes.name: publish_video
scopes.why_we_need_it: We generate short-form video Reels (Kling AI, 9:16, 3–60s, MP4 H.264) and publish them to the user's Page Reels feed via the /{page-id}/video_reels endpoint (three-phase resumable upload). Reels are the highest-engagement format on Facebook in 2026 — the single most-requested feature from our customers.

helper_scopes_not_requiring_advanced_acc.name: instagram_basic
helper_scopes_not_requiring_advanced_acc.why: Granted automatically with instagram_content_publish — we use it to read the IG Business account ID off the Page (the instagram_business_account field on /me/accounts) so we know which IG user to publish to. No app review needed for this scope.

scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.name: pages_read_user_content
scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.currently_in_FLB_configuration: [VERIFY] check the FLB Configuration on developers.facebook.com
scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.decision: REMOVE from both before submitting. We do not actually read user comments or messages — leaving this in the OAuth string risks a rejection on grounds of 'requested scope not demonstrated in use case'.
scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.action_required: Before recording: edit src/services/facebookService.ts to remove pages_read_user_content from the classic-mode scope string on line ~56. Also open the FLB Configuration and remove it from the permissions list.

scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.name: read_insights
scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.why_not: Our app degrades gracefully when this is not granted — see facebookService.ts:170 (the try/catch around the insights endpoint). pages_read_engagement covers the same use case at the post level. Adding read_insights would expand scope without adding user value.

scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.name: business_management
scopes_explicitly_NOT_being_submitted.why_not: We don't operate at the Business Manager level. Users connect individual Pages they admin.
